domain_experience: understanding where mid-sized cities waste money
network_access: industry familiarity but employer customers are off-limits
constraints: cannot rely on confidential employer materials
unfair_advantages: publicly usable edge is domain fluency and problem selection, not employer-confidential information

binding_constraints: Do not use employer confidential information, internal templates, pricing logic, or customer-specific workflow knowledge.
Do not solicit current employer customers.
A side business must be scoped to public information and original work product.
Limited time and no-consulting preference make immediate revenue unlikely.

opportunities.title: Public procurement teardown reports for adjacent buyers
opportunities.thesis: Sell fixed-scope teardown memos based only on public procurement records to adjacent buyers outside the employer's customer set.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can identify waste patterns faster than a generalist because of real domain experience.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: It resembles consulting, needs active selling, and creates conflict-screening overhead.
validation_plan: Draft one sample teardown using only public records.
Test demand with 3 adjacent buyers outside employer customer accounts.
Ask for one paid pilot at $250-$500.
kill_criteria: Stop if the offer requires custom advisory work beyond 5 hours per week.
Stop if conflict review remains unclear after agreement review.

opportunities.title: Public-records procurement benchmark and template library
opportunities.thesis: Build a subscription product or paid library that benchmarks procurement waste signals using public records and original templates created from scratch.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows which procurement signals matter and can turn messy public documents into practical benchmarks.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Distribution is weak, monetization may be slow, and the scope must stay clean-room from employer IP.
validation_plan: Compile 25 public procurement records from 5 mid-sized cities.
Create 1 benchmark memo and 3 original templates by Day 4.
Run 6 demand conversations with a single narrow ICP.
Make 2 pre-sell asks for a $99 template pack or $250 quarterly subscription.
tool_specs: Dataset of public procurement events and waste flags
Template library in Notion, PDF, or static site
LLM workflow for summarizing and tagging public records
kill_criteria: Stop if agreement review shows the product is prohibited.
Stop if the product cannot be defined using only public information and original work product.
Stop or pivot if fewer than 4 of 6 conversations confirm the same pain.
Stop or pivot if 0 of 2 pre-sell asks convert within 14 days.

opportunities.title: Niche newsletter plus paid workflow packs for public buyers
opportunities.thesis: Publish procurement waste analyses and sell companion workflow packs built from public law, public documents, and original frameworks.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has better editorial judgment than generic commentators because of domain fluency.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Audience-building is slow and weakly suited to 5 hours per week without existing distribution.
validation_plan: Write 2 sample issues aimed at one ICP.
Test 10 outbound messages to non-conflicted recipients.
Ask 2 respondents to pay for a starter template pack.
kill_criteria: Stop if fewer than 3 of 10 targets engage.
Stop if content production exceeds the weekly time budget.

opportunities.title: Public benchmark memos to benchmark subscription
opportunities.thesis: Start with a very small number of paid benchmark memos for non-conflicted buyers, then standardize recurring insights into a benchmark database and subscription.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This uses domain judgment without depending on employer customer access.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The service-first wedge still conflicts with the user's preference and may be too slow with only 5 hours per week.
validation_plan: Sell 1 paid benchmark memo to a non-conflicted buyer.
Identify repeated fields and convert them into a reusable benchmark format.
Ask pilot users if they want quarterly updates or searchable comparisons.
kill_criteria: Stop if no pilot buyer is willing to pay for a memo.
Stop software plans if no buyer requests recurring access.

rejected_paths.title: Directly repackaging employer templates, pricing logic, or workflow insights
rejected_paths.reason: Highest legal and confidentiality risk and directly conflicts with the requirement to stay fully legal.

rejected_paths.title: Broad procurement consulting
rejected_paths.reason: Conflicts with the user's stated preference and exceeds the weekly time budget.

rejected_paths.title: Software-first govtech micro-SaaS
rejected_paths.reason: Too much build before proof of demand and current distribution is too weak.

next_actions: Review employment agreement clauses for confidentiality, moonlighting, invention assignment, and non-solicit restrictions.
Choose one narrow ICP outside employer customer accounts.
Build a clean-room prototype from public records only.
Run 6 validation conversations and 2 pre-sell asks within 7 days.
Proceed only if pain and willingness to pay are both confirmed.
